methods.description: Process for creating a Digital Terrain Model (DTM) using Soviet topographic maps and correcting it for changes due to human activities.

steps.description: The DTM was created, using 1 : 10 000 and 1 : 25 000 Soviet topographic maps.
steps.searchable_summary: create DTM from Soviet topographic maps

steps.description: The thickness of the cultural layer was removed, according to maps compiled by Zobel (2008).
steps.searchable_summary: remove cultural layer thickness from DTM

steps.description: To correct the DTM elevation and coastline, the Russian maps from the end of the 19th century were also used.
steps.searchable_summary: correct DTM elevation and coastline using 19th-century Russian maps

methods.description: Derivation of interpolated water level surfaces using point kriging approach from coastal formation databases.

steps.description: The interpolated water level surfaces were derived from the BIL, Ancylus Lake and Litorina Sea coastal formation databases.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch water level data from coastal formation databases

steps.description: Using a point kriging approach, interpolated surfaces were created.
steps.searchable_summary: perform point kriging to interpolate water level surfaces

steps.description: Residuals, i.e., the difference of the actual site elevation from the interpolated surface, were calculated to find out the erroneous data and omit sites with residuals more than 1 m.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate residuals and filter out erroneous data

methods.description: Modelling palaeoreconstructions for different time slices based on water level fluctuation curves and assumptions about land uplift.

steps.description: Palaeoreconstructions for 4400, 3000, 2000 and 1000 cal yr BP were based on the water level fluctuation curve compiled for the Tallinn area.
steps.searchable_summary: model palaeoreconstructions using water level fluctuation curve

steps.description: Assuming that land uplift decreased linearly after the Litorina Sea transgression.
steps.searchable_summary: apply linear land uplift assumption to models

methods.description: Calibration of radiocarbon dates used to estimate the age of different stages of the Baltic Sea.

steps.description: The radiocarbon dates were calibrated according to the calibration data set from Reimer et al. (2004) and CALIB5.0.1 software.
inputs: Reimer et al. (2004) calibration data set
